text: unnatural angles . The others, damaged but not incapacitated, returned fire. Bullets cracked into the face of the building, breaking the ancient bricks into dust. Glass shattered, and the gunshots came so quickly that they were almost indistinguishable from one another. Ronin fired into Northside as the gearheads retreated toward the trees . Calculating Northside’s movement, Ronin squeezed the trigger again. The rifle bucked, and five-hundredths of a second later, the bullet tore through the gearhead’s chest. Blue fire sprayed from its back and eyes, and it fell to the pavement, smoking . Several of the remaining gearheads fired bursts up at the fourth floor. Cracking brick and splintering wood dominated Ronin’s audio receptors as the rounds ripped through the wall, spraying debris across the room. Several rounds connected with his casing. Only two breached — a partial penetration on his right thigh and a full one over his left hip. Diagnostics reported mobility loss due to the damage, flashing alerts across his system. He dismissed them; such damage could be addressed later . Dozer released a frustrated growl and turned sharply away from the window, lifting a hand to her face. Shrapnel had damaged her left optic. Muttering another curse, she raised her rifle and fired a burst out the window . Ronin returned to his position, shifting his weapon to his left hand to compensate for his inability to twist his hips beyond a few degrees . The gearheads scrambled beyond the tree line. Bullets caught the trunks, shredding wood, and kicked up clods of dirt and grass below. The gearheads fired blindly from behind their cover. On the road, more figures approached . They weren’t friendly reinforcements. No, it was too early for that, and it was probably too much to hope for, anyway. Warlord owned most of the guns in Cheyenne . “Won’t be long before they try to flank us,” Ronin said, “if they haven’t already .” Dozer’s gaze was grim and unwavering. “I haven’t used you as a bullet shield yet. Maybe that’ll be my chance .” Below, at least twelve more gearheads joined their eight damaged comrades. With both sides of the conflict under some sort of cover, a hail of bullets would only waste ammunition. Alpha Team’s supply wouldn’t last through a day-long firefight. How many bullets had Warlord stockpiled ? “ Fuck .” Ronin turned his head to Ramirez. Pale-faced, the soldier gritted his teeth and pressed a hand to his abdomen. Blood oozed over it . “That doesn’t look good,” Dozer said quietly. She fired off three more rounds in quick succession . Ramirez leaned back into the wall and slid down it onto the floor, leaving a streak of blood behind him . Ronin and Jensen rushed to the wounded soldier, kneeling on either side of him . “Shit, Ramirez…shit! Okay. We got McGowan downstairs, he can patch you up until we get you to the Doc.” Jensen scrambled to his feet. “I’ll get him, and you’ll be okay . Just — ” Ronin grabbed Jensen’s sleeve, halting him . “What, man? We gotta get help for him !” Leaning forward, Ronin met Ramirez’s gaze. The youth’s breathing was shallow. Sweat rolled down his face from beneath his helmet . “Fuck, it hurts,” Ramirez said through his teeth . “I know,” Ronin replied. “Just focus on me and breathe. You can’t stay here, Ramirez .” “No, no. I can’t walk.” Ramirez squeezed his eyes shut. He shook his head with weak desperation. “Hurts too much .” “It’s going to hurt more soon, but you need to stay with me. The next bullet that comes through this wall will kill you if we don’t move .” “We shouldn’t move him.” Jensen rubbed a hand over his face, smearing dirt across his cheek . No one came out of the Dust clean. No one . More gunshots boomed outside. Ronin laid the ancient rifle on the floor. “Help is downstairs .” Ramirez shook his head again. He didn’t open his eyes, didn’t slow his breathing . Ronin’s processors raced, running through a myriad of possibilities. Most of their options ended with the young soldier’s probable death. But Ronin’s memory kept turning to the dark rooms downstairs. Rooms that were adapted for human care. If any of that equipment was still functional … Ronin slipped one arm beneath Ramirez’s legs and the other around his back. Something ground and stuttered in his hip. The soldier screamed, clutching at Ronin with one hand, keeping the other pressed over his gut. Ronin stood . Jensen muttered curses, pacing restlessly . “Jensen, get your ass to a window and return fire!” Dozer shouted . After a brief hesitation, Jensen obeyed, throwing his shoulder against the wall beside Ramirez’s narrow window. His rifle roared — its thunder matched by Ramirez’s screams — while Ronin carried the wounded soldier into the stairwell . Ramirez quieted as they moved down . “Still with me, Ramirez?” Ronin asked, adjusting his suspension systems to keep the wounded soldier as still as possible . “ Sorry …” “For what?” They passed the doorway to the third floor . “What I said about your girl,” Ramirez replied, his voice strained. “Wasn’t right .” Ronin’s hip locked, causing his foot to come down hard on the second floor landing . “Fuck.” Ramirez clenched his jaw “I promise that wasn’t retaliation,” Ronin said, without humor . They emerged on the ground floor. The soldiers in the reception room shouted to one another over the booming rifles, the noise amplified as it echoed down the halls. Ronin carefully set Ramirez on the floor at the rear of the room and maxed his vocalizer, calling for McGowan . The medic was an older model bot — built in humanoid shape, but not styled to mimic a human’s appearance. His casing was dull gray and sleek, with gangly limbs.
